purpose: Add the non-KRITIS security and architecture invariant hardening required for Ananta as an OSS AI control plane. This track focuses on state ownership, policy enforcement, transition traceability, thin clients, explicit context and lightweight deterministic reproducibility. It intentionally avoids Enterprise/KRITIS scope such as tenancy, SSO, RBAC, compliance reports, SIEM, WORM storage or runtime attestation.
goal: Prove that core Ananta execution cannot bypass Hub-controlled policy, approval, audit and explicit context boundaries, while keeping the OSS implementation small, testable and usable.
non_goals: Do not implement KRITIS or Enterprise RBAC/ABAC in this track.
Do not implement OIDC/SSO, tenant isolation, SIEM export or compliance reporting here.
Do not implement WORM storage, signed audit ledgers, hardware attestation or formal regulated evidence packages here.
Do not build a full replay lab or deterministic VM/container capture system.
Do not block OSS usability with heavyweight enterprise controls.
success_criteria: Every core state type has one declared owner and controlled writers.
All execution-like paths go through one policy and approval gatekeeper.
Goal, plan, task, approval, execution, artifact, verification and repair transitions are traceable with correlation IDs.
Clients can submit requests and render states but cannot orchestrate, approve or execute directly.
Context passed to models, workers or tools is explicit, bounded and provenance-aware.
Artifacts and runs contain enough hashes, references and metadata for lightweight reproducibility.
Tests fail on policy bypass, implicit context, client-side orchestration or missing trace metadata.
Release gate can include these checks without requiring KRITIS infrastructure.
architecture_guardrails: Hub remains the single authority for orchestration, policy, approval and audit.
Worker and tool execution must never be reachable from clients directly.
Repair and retry paths must be treated as execution paths and pass through the same gatekeeper.
Audit/trace must be append-oriented and must not silently overwrite prior decisions.
Context must be explicit: no hidden project scan, implicit global path use or unprovenanced RAG chunks.
Deterministic reproducibility is lightweight in OSS: record refs, hashes and versions, not full regulated replay infrastructure.
KRITIS hardening can extend this track later but must not be required for the OSS golden path.

tasks.title: State ownership matrix define
acceptance_criteria: Matrix declares owner, allowed writers and readers for goal, plan, task, execution, approval, artifact, audit, verification, repair and client UI state.
Every fachlicher/core state has exactly one owner.
Clients are not writers for server-owned state except through public API requests.
Document explains that audit is append-oriented and not ordinary mutable state.

tasks.title: State ownership validation tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify each core state has exactly one owner.
Tests verify writer lists are explicit and non-empty where mutation is possible.
Tests fail on unknown state types, duplicate owners or client-owned server state.
Tests are included in the OSS release gate.

tasks.title: Core invariant checklist document add
acceptance_criteria: Checklist covers state ownership, policy gate, transition traceability, thin clients, explicit context and lightweight reproducibility.
Checklist is written for OSS maintainers and AI coding agents.
Checklist explicitly separates OSS invariants from KRITIS/Enterprise enhancements.

tasks.title: Single execution gatekeeper define
acceptance_criteria: Document defines one required gatekeeper path for execution-like actions: validate context, resolve capability, evaluate policy, check approval, audit pre-event, execute, audit result and store artifact.
Repair, retry, tool execution, worker execution and domain action execution are explicitly classified as execution-like paths.
Document forbids trusted shortcuts and direct client-to-worker execution.

tasks.title: Policy bypass negative tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify representative execution paths call policy evaluation before execution.
Tests cover normal execution, repair execution, retry execution and domain action execution if available.
Tests verify denied policy does not fall back to direct worker/tool execution.
Tests fail when a new execution path is registered without policy enforcement metadata.

tasks.title: Approval binding tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify approval is bound to the specific action/capability/context hash it approved.
Tests cover stale approval, malformed approval, approval for different action and missing approval.
Tests verify approval-required actions cannot execute with only a client-side approval flag.
Tests verify repair paths also require fresh approval where policy requires it.

tasks.title: Transition event schema add
acceptance_criteria: Schema supports goal_created, plan_proposed, task_created, approval_requested, approval_decided, execution_started, execution_finished, execution_failed, artifact_created, verification_finished and repair_proposed.
Schema requires event_id, timestamp, correlation_id, causation_id, actor/system component, state type and entity id.
Schema supports compact metadata without storing sensitive full context by default.
Schema is versioned and backwards-compatible for basic extension.

tasks.title: Transition trace consistency tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify core transitions emit correlation IDs and causation IDs.
Tests verify execution result events reference the matching execution start event.
Tests verify artifacts reference task/execution IDs.
Tests detect missing or orphaned transition events in golden-path flows.

tasks.title: Append-oriented audit minimum document and tests add
acceptance_criteria: Document defines OSS audit minimum and explicitly excludes WORM/tamper-evident KRITIS storage.
Tests verify audit/trace events are appended for policy denial, approval request, approval decision and execution result.
Tests verify audit events avoid raw secrets and excessive full context by default.

tasks.title: Client thinness rules and tests add
acceptance_criteria: Rules define that clients may collect bounded context, submit requests and render Hub states but may not orchestrate, approve, evaluate policy or execute tools directly.
Tests or audit scripts check representative client surfaces for forbidden imports or direct execution calls where practical.
Tests verify clients handle denied, approval-required and degraded states rather than transforming them into local actions.
Rules cover CLI, TUI, Neovim, Eclipse and future Blender/VS Code surfaces.

tasks.title: ContextEnvelope v1 schema add
acceptance_criteria: Schema defines explicit_inputs, user_selection, retrieved_context, source_refs, redactions, size_limits, context_hash and provenance metadata.
Schema distinguishes user-provided context, client-captured context and RAG-retrieved context.
Schema forbids unlabelled raw context blobs for execution-critical paths.
Schema can be reused by domain integrations such as Blender, FreeCAD and KiCad.

tasks.title: Implicit context negative tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify model/worker/tool requests require a ContextEnvelope or equivalent explicit context metadata.
Tests verify RAG chunks include source refs and retrieval reasons.
Tests verify likely secrets or unrelated files are not silently included in context.
Tests verify oversized or malformed context is rejected or degraded explicitly.

tasks.title: TraceBundle v1 lightweight schema add
acceptance_criteria: Schema records goal_id, plan_id, task_ids, model id where applicable, prompt template version, context_hash, RAG refs, policy decisions, approval decisions and artifact hashes.
Schema supports lightweight replay/debugging without requiring full KRITIS replay infrastructure.
Schema avoids embedding secrets or full large contexts by default.
Schema can be attached to release/golden-path artifacts.

tasks.title: Artifact provenance and hash tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify artifacts include task/execution reference, artifact type, provenance summary and content hash or equivalent stable digest.
Tests verify generated scripts/plans include source context refs where RAG influenced them.
Tests verify artifact metadata is enough to connect artifact to trace bundle.
Tests avoid requiring Enterprise-grade signatures or immutable storage.

tasks.title: OSS security invariant release gate add
acceptance_criteria: One documented command runs OSS security invariant checks.
Release gate includes these checks as blocking for runtime/merge readiness claims.
Gate output separates OSS invariant failures from deferred KRITIS/Enterprise hardening.
Gate fails on policy bypass, missing state owner, direct client execution, implicit context or missing artifact provenance.

execution_order_summary: Stage 1: Define state ownership and core invariant model.
Stage 2: Define and test the single execution gatekeeper and approval binding.
Stage 3: Add transition event and audit minimum traceability.
Stage 4: Enforce thin clients and explicit ContextEnvelope usage.
Stage 5: Add lightweight TraceBundle, artifact provenance and release-gate integration.

notes: tasks_status_summary.by_risk wurde auf die realen Task-Risiken korrigiert (medium=1, high=6, critical=8).
execution_stage_summary wurde ergänzt, damit Milestone-Rollups transparent und maschinenlesbar sind.
updated_at/checked_at wurden auf den aktuellen Analysezeitpunkt gesetzt.
Keine fehlenden Task-Referenzen in milestones, depends_on oder critical_path_tasks gefunden.
Bundle SEC-M1 wurde umgesetzt: state_ownership matrix/docs und zugehoerige Validierungstests sind vorhanden.

summary_notes: This is the OSS-core version of security hardening, not the KRITIS/Enterprise track.
The goal is to make Ananta structurally safe enough for local-first OSS usage without pulling in heavyweight compliance infrastructure.
KRITIS can later extend these invariants with RBAC, SSO, tenancy, signed audit, WORM storage, SIEM export and regulated evidence packages.
This track should run after client runtime tests and before large domain integrations create additional execution paths.
Status counters and milestone stage rollups are now synchronized and explicitly documented.
SEC-M1 is implemented as the first completed bundle and establishes the ownership baseline for follow-up policy and trace tasks.

end_summaries.title: Analyseergebnis
items: Die Datei ist strukturell konsistent aufgebaut (Milestones, Dependencies und Critical Path referenzieren nur vorhandene Tasks).
Ein Drift wurde in tasks_status_summary.by_risk gefunden (vorher nicht deckungsgleich mit den tatsächlichen Task-Risiken).

end_summaries.title: Korrekturen
items: by_risk korrigiert auf low=0, medium=1, high=6, critical=8.
updated_at in tasks_status_summary auf den aktuellen Analysezeitpunkt gesetzt.

end_summaries.title: Verbesserungen und Erweiterungen
items: analysis_checks ergänzt, damit Prüfergebnis und Korrekturhistorie nachvollziehbar dokumentiert sind.

end_summaries.title: Status am Ende
items: Aktueller Umsetzungsstand: 12 todo, 0 in_progress, 0 blocked, 3 done (20.0%).
SEC-M1 ist done; verbleibend sind SEC-M2 bis SEC-M5 mit unveränderter kritischer Pfadlänge von 13 Tasks (2 erledigt, 11 offen).
